name: Beef Chow Fun
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Beef flank steak, sliced thin across the grain
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the marinade by combining 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 1 egg white, and 1 tablespoon peanut oil.
2. Set aside additional ingredients for saucing: 7 tablespoons peanut oil, 2 cloves garlic (chopped fine), 1 slice ginger (cut julienne), 1 tablespoon fermented black beans (rinsed), 1 tablespoon dry sherry, half a yellow onion (peeled and sliced), and half an green sweet bell pepper (cored and cut julienne).
3. In a separate bowl, combine 1/4 teaspoon MSG (optional), 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce, 1/4 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon dry sherry, 1 pinch white pepper, and 1 tablespoon oyster sauce for the final sauce.
4. Heat the wok with 2 tablespoons peanut oil, toss the noodles until very hot and beginning to color slightly on the edges. Remove to serving platter and set aside.
5. Add 3 tablespoons peanut oil to the wok, sauté garlic and ginger briefly, then add black beans and sherry. Cook onions and green pepper until very hot.
6. Return vegetables to serving platter, heat wok again with 2 tablespoons peanut oil, brown meat for one side only.
7. Add noodles and sauce mixture back to the wok with meat, toss until heated through.
8. Add bean sprouts, toss briefly, garnish with Chinese parsley before serving.
Note: Avoid aggressive culinary terms such as "burn," "splutter," or "execute."
